learning_outcomes.description: Apply ethical considerations underpinning research in a health project.
Critically evaluate, transform, and interpret information in the context of the existing knowledge base and translational implications of the findings.
Demonstrate effective academic writing in the production of a research report suitable for publication in a peer-reviewed journal.
Discuss and justify the research methods, and conclusions in defence of the research findings.
Interpret and transmit research findings to specialist and non-specialist audiences.
Develop and demonstrate the teamwork capabilities required to collaboratively construct a rigorous research project.

The first group task focuses on ethical governance and data collection status.

The second task is an individual interview to assess each student’s understanding and contribution to the project.

The final group interview evaluates project advancement and readiness for completion.
assessments.task_description:
This assessment stream consists of three progress interviews designed to support and monitor the development of the research project. Conducted at key points across the course, these tasks provide opportunities for feedback on project governance, individual and group engagement, and overall progress.

The first group task focuses on ethical governance and data collection status.

The second task is an individual interview to assess each student’s understanding and contribution to the project.

The final group interview evaluates project advancement and readiness for completion.

These tasks aim to assist with project management, support successful completion of the research project and ensure individual accountability for the project.

Performance parameters, and assessment criteria and standards will be made available on the course Blackboard site prior to the assessment
assessments.submission_guidelines: Assessment submissions will be organised at times during the stated date ranges.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Research project milestone: Data analysis plan
assessments.task_description:
This is a research project group task.

A structured document outlining how collected data will be prepared, managed, and analysed. This includes proposed quantitative statistical methods or qualitative framework that will be utilised. The purpose of the task is assess students' justification of approaches, and alignment with their specific research project questions and objectives.

Performance parameters, and assessment criteria and standards will be made available on the course Blackboard site prior to the assessment
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Research project milestone: Research project report
assessments.task_description:
A comprehensive written report of the full research project, including:

Title

Abstract

Introduction

Methods

Results

Discussion

This is the capstone project for your Research Informed Practice courses.

Performance parameters, and assessment criteria and standards will be made available on the course Blackboard site prior to the assessment

assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Research project milestone: Final research project presentation
assessments.task_description:
Students will deliver an oral presentation summarising their research project. The presentation should clearly outline the background and rationale for the study, including justification for the chosen study design and methods. Students should then present the results, interpret and discuss their findings, and provide clear conclusions and implications arising from the project.

Students should also highlight and reflect on their contribution to the research project. Following the presentation, students will be asked questions to clarify aspects of the work and demonstrate their understanding of the study design, methods, results, and conclusions.

Performance parameters, and assessment criteria and standards will be made available on the course Blackboard site prior to the assessment.

The presentation will be recorded and retained as per university policy. The recording will be stored in a secure manner and will only be accessed if required for the purposes of moderation of marking; provision of feedback to the student; and/or re-marking following a successful re-mark application.

assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: Students will present a brief summary of their research project to their cohort and members of the public. The presentation should outline the background and rationale for the study, including brief description for the study design and methods. Students should present the key results, interpret and discuss their findings, and provide conclusions and implications arising from the project.

learning_activities.topic:
Research (Self Directed)

Students undertake an independent research project under the guidance and supervision of academic staff. Through regular meetings and feedback, students develop their research and subsequently prepare a written research report that presents their methods, findings, and conclusions.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L04, L05, L06
